purpose: Phase 19 Lead Magnet / Capture Engine for Matrix Reprogrammed. This defines genuinely useful public opt-in routes that connect free briefs, evidence trails, trust pages, offers, campaigns, share kits, book routes, and reader outcomes into email-capture journeys.
rules: Lead magnet pages must not make claims stronger than their linked evidence pages.
Every opt-in page must include a trust route and privacy route.
Every opt-in page must include a clear promise and a public route after signup.
Lead magnets are public download assets, not private subscriber data.
Netlify forms must use unique names for every lead magnet.
Capture pages should move readers from free value into offer and book routes without hiding source boundaries.
Every free brief must give the reader a useful checklist, source path, reader outcome, and next action without requiring payment first.
Every free brief must explain what it helps the reader do, what evidence route to open next, and what paid route logically follows.

magnets.title: The Black File Starter Brief
magnets.label: Starter opt-in
magnets.summary: A practical entry brief for readers who want the Matrix Reprogrammed map, Epstein/elite-file evidence boundary, Black File route, and first book path without getting lost in the archive.
magnets.promise: Get the clean starting route into the archive: what to open first, how to separate evidence from theory, and how to follow the Black File trail without losing the source boundary.
magnets.readerOutcome: The reader leaves knowing where to start, which files matter first, what evidence class means, and which deeper book route to open next.
whatYouLearn: The difference between a document trail, a public allegation, a court finding, a contact record, and speculation.
How to enter the Matrix Reprogrammed archive without bouncing between disconnected pages.
Which public pages lead to the Epstein Command Center, Evidence Vault, books, videos, and offers.
Why the Black File is the deeper reading route after the free public record path.
checklist: Open the Evidence Vault first.
Read the Evidence Boundary before drawing conclusions.
Use the Epstein Command Center as the live case board.
Move from public records into the Black File only after the source route is clear.
Use the store/book route when you want the full dossier, not just the map.
actionSteps: Choose the starter-library offer if you want the full system path.
magnets.nextBestStep: Read the Black File or enter the Starter Library offer.

magnets.title: D.O.G Architect Initiation Brief
magnets.label: D.O.G opt-in
magnets.summary: A symbolic reader brief for D.O.G The Architect: how to read the collector-volume route, understand symbolic power, and avoid confusing esoteric interpretation with evidence claims.
magnets.promise: Get the D.O.G reading route: what the book is, how to read its symbolic architecture, what the plates mean, and where interpretation stops being proof.
magnets.readerOutcome: The reader understands the D.O.G brand, collector-volume logic, symbolic reading method, and why the paid book is the deeper experience.
whatYouLearn: How D.O.G The Architect fits inside the Matrix Reprogrammed book universe.
How to read symbolism, plate language, ritual architecture, and esoteric commentary responsibly.
Why claim labels matter when occult interpretation meets public history.
Where the collector offer, book page, and symbolic-power campaign connect.
checklist: Open the D.O.G book page.
Read the claim-label boundary.
Separate symbolic meaning from factual allegation.
Use the collector offer if you want the full hardback/volume route.
Share only labelled symbolic analysis, not unsupported claims.
magnets.nextBestStep: Open the D.O.G Architect collector offer.

magnets.title: Intelligence Files Starter Brief
magnets.label: Agency opt-in
magnets.summary: A declassified-history starter brief for readers entering the intelligence dossiers, agency maps, source-methodology pages, and public-record archive routes.
magnets.promise: Get the agency archive route: how to enter declassified files, separate official record from inference, and move into the intelligence dossier reading path.
magnets.readerOutcome: The reader can start researching agency files without treating every declassified fragment as a complete conclusion.
whatYouLearn: Which archive routes matter first: declassified files, oversight records, agency releases, and public dockets.
How to separate source, inference, allegation, and conclusion.
How the Intelligence Dossiers series is organised as a reading path.
Why the source-methodology page is the safety rail for darker material.
checklist: Open the declassified archive evidence lane.
Read the source-methodology page.
Pick one agency dossier first.
Map claim strength before sharing.
Use the Intelligence offer route for the complete path.
actionSteps: Move into the Intelligence Dossiers offer.
magnets.nextBestStep: Enter the Intelligence Dossiers offer path.

magnets.title: Crime Network Starter Brief
magnets.label: Crime dossier opt-in
magnets.summary: A practical reader brief for organized crime, cartel logistics, court records, sanctions, laundering routes, and crime-state overlap pages.
magnets.promise: Get the crime-network route while keeping association, indictment, sanction, conviction, and speculation boundaries clear.
magnets.readerOutcome: The reader knows how to follow crime-network records without turning association or investigation into automatic guilt.
whatYouLearn: How court records, indictments, sanctions, convictions, and reporting differ.
How to follow logistics, laundering, state protection, and cartel/mafia overlap.
Which book path covers crime networks in greater depth.
Why association boundaries matter when names appear in criminal cases.
checklist: Open the court-record evidence lane.
Check whether the record is charge, conviction, sanction, allegation, or association.
Map logistics, money, protection, and violence separately.
Open the Crime Dossiers page.
Move into the crime offer if you want the complete series route.
magnets.nextBestStep: Read the Crime Dossiers route or open the Crime Dossier offer.

magnets.title: Trust And Evidence Checklist
magnets.label: Credibility opt-in
magnets.summary: A credibility checklist for source hierarchy, claim labels, corrections, association boundaries, evidence-vault navigation, and safe reading of darker public-record material.
magnets.promise: Get the source-discipline checklist before entering the darker archive routes: how to judge claim strength, source hierarchy, and association boundaries.
magnets.readerOutcome: The reader can navigate controversial material without confusing source, theory, claim, and conclusion.
whatYouLearn: The difference between primary source, public reporting, archive lead, claim, and speculation.
How corrections, privacy, and trust pages protect the reader and the site.
How to judge whether a page is making a claim or asking a research question.
Which evidence vault route to open when a number or allegation matters.
checklist: Identify the claim type.
Find the strongest available source.
Check whether a person is central, peripheral, accused, convicted, or merely mentioned.
Use the corrections route if something needs review.
Do not share a claim stronger than the evidence route supports.
actionSteps: Use the checklist before entering darker pages.
magnets.nextBestStep: Open the Trust Center before continuing into the darker archive.

magnets.title: Full Archive Map
magnets.label: Full-system opt-in
magnets.summary: A broad but useful map for readers who want books, offers, launches, share kits, feeds, download packs, evidence routes, and the full Matrix Reprogrammed reading order in one pathway.
magnets.promise: Get the full map of the Matrix Reprogrammed archive: books, evidence lanes, free briefs, live intel, offers, and the cleanest route through them.
magnets.readerOutcome: The reader gets a complete navigation map rather than a random pile of pages.
whatYouLearn: Where to start if you are new to the site.
Which book series fits which interest: intelligence, crime, symbolic power, survival, trust, or Black File.
How free briefs, offers, downloads, videos, and live intel connect.
Which trust/evidence pages to check before entering controversial material.
checklist: Start at the Book Universe.
Choose one reader path.
Open the Evidence Vault for source context.
Use the Launch Room for campaigns.
Use the Offer Center when ready to buy.
magnets.nextBestStep: Open the Book Universe and choose the reader path that fits your interest.
